name: SurgeTrader
description_short: SurgeTrader was a proprietary trading firm offering funded accounts up to $1M with a single-phase audition and up to 75% profit splits for forex and commodities traders.
description_long:
SurgeTrader was a US-based proprietary trading firm that distinguished itself by offering a streamlined one-step evaluation process — called an "Audition" — rather than the multi-phase challenges common among competitors. Traders could access funded accounts ranging from $25,000 to $1,000,000 after demonstrating consistent profitability and disciplined risk management during the evaluation phase.

The firm supported trading on forex pairs, commodities (including gold and oil), and indices via MetaTrader 4 and MetaTrader 5 platforms. Automated trading via Expert Advisors (EAs) was permitted, making it appealing to algorithmic traders who wanted a funded environment without the overhead of running their own capital. Profit splits reached up to 75% in favor of the funded trader.

SurgeTrader operated with relatively straightforward rules: a 10% maximum drawdown limit, a 4–5% daily loss limit depending on account tier, and a 10% profit target to pass the Audition. There was no minimum trading day requirement, which was a notable advantage for traders with flexible schedules. Challenge fees were one-time payments with no monthly subscription required once funded.

SurgeTrader ceased operations in mid-2023, citing regulatory and operational pressures that affected several prop firms during that period. Traders with active funded accounts were notified of the wind-down. While the firm had positive reviews for its simple ruleset and fast payouts during its operational period, its closure highlighted the risks of engaging with newer or smaller prop firms in an evolving regulatory landscape.
pros: Single-phase evaluation simplifies the path to a funded account
No minimum trading day requirement gives schedule flexibility
Automated and EA trading explicitly permitted
Accounts up to $1M available for experienced traders
One-time evaluation fee with no recurring monthly cost
cons: Ceased operations in mid-2023, no longer accepting new traders
Profit split of 75% was competitive but not industry-leading
Limited market selection compared to multi-asset prop firms
No educational resources or trading tools provided
best_for: forex traders seeking simple evaluation rules
traders wanting large funded account sizes
